feed.title: International Journal of the Sociology of Language
feed.subtitle: Latest articles from International Journal of the Sociology of Language (De Gruyter)

entry.title: The revivals of Manx – introduction
entry.summary: Authors: Christopher Lewin, Erin McNulty DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2026-0002 Pages: Page range: 1-22

entry.title: Manx language for all: community, cooperation and creativity in the 21st century
entry.summary: Authors: Breesha Maddrell, Ruth Keggin Gell DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0036 Pages: Page range: 23-55

entry.title: Rationalisation versus revitalisation: neoliberal capitalism and language revival in the Isle of Man
entry.summary: Authors: Ben Ó Ceallaigh DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0047 Pages: Page range: 57-81

entry.title: What does it mean to speak “good Manx”? Insights from ideologies and attitudes around Manx morphosyntax
entry.summary: Authors: Erin McNulty DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0035 Pages: Page range: 83-115

entry.title: Pan-Gaelicism and the perception of the Gaelicness of Manx in the revivalist founder group
entry.summary: Authors: Tadhg Ó hIfearnáin DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0037 Pages: Page range: 117-145

entry.title: Digitising Manx language recordings: Manx National Heritage and the British Library’s ‘Unlocking our Sound Heritage’ project
entry.summary: Authors: Nicola Tooms DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0055 Pages: Page range: 147-161

entry.title: Constructing “Traditional Manx”: the persistence of native speaker models in post-shift revitalization
entry.summary: Authors: Christopher Lewin DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0030 Pages: Page range: 163-188

entry.title: Models of language revitalisation around the world: processes and outcomes
entry.summary: Authors: Julia Sallabank DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijsl-2025-0057 Pages: Page range: 189-228
